author: Qazi Akmal
title: Kitab-us-Siyim
gloss: A booklet in which injunctions about fasting are interpreted for the guidance of Ahmadis, from the point of view of a follower of Mirza Ghulam Ahmad of Qádián.
notes: Dual date: 1328 Hijri / 1st September 1910. Ahmadi fiqh from Qadian's own press.

author: Satya Dhari
title: Mi'yar-i-Sadaqat
gloss: For subject see entry No. 39 at page 41 of the catalogue for the quarter ending the 30th September, 1910.
copyright: S. Sat Dhari, Lahore; No. (?), dated the (?) September, 1910.
notes: Cross-reference within this same quarter's catalogue (entry 39, page 41). 552 pages.

flags.issue: register number and day partially legible
self-referential cross-reference — target entry No. 39 p41 is in this quarter's Urdu—Poetry (Durar-i-Samin?); verify at adjudication

author: Shahzada Ram, Lala
title: Mukti ka Satya Gyan
gloss: A pamphlet dealing with such subjects as God, the soul, pure-living, &c., in the light of the Arya Samaj tenets.

title: Sharait-i-Baia't
gloss: For subject see entry No. 48 at page 36 of the catalogue for the quarter ending the 30th June, 1910.
notes: Cross-reference: 1910Q2 entry 48, page 36 (our Q2 extraction). Conditions of bai'at (initiation) into the Ahmadiyya.

author: Sharf
title: Sitaish wa Tárif ki Kitab
gloss: A tract in which the writer deals with the sacredness of the Zabur or the Book of Psalms, which he thinks the oldest inspired book.

author: Shiv Brat Lal, M.A., Babu
title: Upanishad
gloss: An Urdu translation of the ten principal Upanishads, with short explanatory notes here and there.
notes: Ten principal Upanishads in Urdu, 576 pp. — third Shiv Brat Lal title this quarter (with p27 s39, p37 s122).
marks: X; pencil '88' right margin; illegible cursive pencil note lower left margin (reads like 'with … not sandpath I')

title: Zabur ki Kitab ke Chand Mazamir ka Tarmim Shuda Tarjama, Hissa-i-Duwum
gloss: A translation in Urdu of certain Psalms of David, Part II by Rev. H. U. Wightbrecht, Ph. D., D. D.
notes: Same missionary translator as p45 s66; 100 copies only.

author: Muhammad Muazzam, Maulvi
title: Islam ki Pahli Kitab
gloss: The first book of Islam containing preliminary religious precepts for Muhammadan boys.
notes: Entry preceded by '(Designed for educational purposes.)'.
